summary: Phase 001 HLD/LLD can feed CocoIndex rerank after it ships, but the right design is a static sidecar export plus disabled-by-default role/proximity boosts that fail closed on stale metadata.

label: HLD/LLD exposes enough signal for role-aware rerank, but consumers must use open-string file_role rather than a closed architectural_role enum
Static HLD sidecar export is lower-coupling than a live Python-to-TypeScript graph DB query inside CocoIndex rerank
Rerank fusion belongs at _ranked_result with small capped score nudges and rankingSignals behind an env flag
Stale HLD sidecars must fail closed using graph/file freshness metadata, not silently apply wrong boosts
Active implementation should defer until Phase 001 ships; keep the post-Phase-001 feature-flag design
